---
title: "Javier Enrique Aguilar"
subtitle: "Postdoctoral Researcher in Bayesian Statistics "
from: markdown+emoji
about:
  template: trestles
  image: graphics/javier-aguilar.jpeg
  image-width: 15em
  image-shape: round

  links:
    - text: Email
      icon: envelope-fill
      href: mailto:javier.aguilarr@icloud.com
    - text: GitHub
      icon: github
      href: https://github.com/jear2412
    - text: Google Scholar
      icon: mortarboard-fill
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ysOrxQUAAAAJ&hl=en


format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
    
aliases:
  - /about
---

::: {#about-heading}


I am a postdoctoral researcher at the [Research Center Trustworthy Data Science and Security](https://rc-trust.ai) working in the Causality group with [Alexander Marx](https://www.a-marx.com). I completed my doctoral degree in Statistics 
under the supervision of [Paul Bürkner](https://paul-buerkner.github.io) at TU Dortmund University. Previously, I was a research assistant at the [Cluster of Excellence SimTech](https://www.simtech.uni-stuttgart.de) at the University of  Stuttgart. I hold a Master's degree in Probability and Statistics from the  [Center for Research in Mathematics (CIMAT)](https://ppe.cimat.mx) in Guanajuato, Mexico, where I was supervised by [Andrés Christen](https://www.cimat.mx/~jac/).

My research focuses on Bayesian computation, prior specification, probabilistic programming, and high-dimensional statistics. I develop interpretable and problem-specific priors that define joint distributions on predictive quantities, offering new ways to control model complexity, improve uncertainty quantification, and enhance model validation through simulation-based techniques. My work has been published in *Bayesian Analysis*, *Statistics and Computing*, and the *Electronic Journal of Statistics*, and is also available as preprints on [arXiv](https://arxiv.org).

I teach and organize workshops on Bayesian data analysis and the use of [`brms`](https://paul-buerkner.github.io/brms/), an R package for Bayesian regression modeling in [`Stan`](https://mc-stan.org/). These have been held at institutions  such as Charité, Freie Universität Berlin, Humboldt University of Berlin, and the University of Bern. I also collaborate with applied researchers in the natural sciences, adapting and implementing Bayesian methods in real-world studies. Additionally, I offer statistical consulting for research projects requiring Bayesian modeling and data analysis.

Feel free to [get in touch](mailto:javier.aguilarr@icloud.com) if you'd like to discuss ideas, potential projects, or just exchange perspectives on Bayesian methods.


:::
